plain_definition: The umbrella name for the group of US anti-money-laundering and financial-recordkeeping statutes administered through FinCEN, requiring banks and other financial institutions -- including, per FinCEN guidance, businesses that qualify as a 'money services business' by administering or exchanging convertible virtual currency -- to register where required, maintain anti-money-laundering programs, keep specified records, and file reports such as suspicious activity reports and currency transaction reports. It is the statutory foundation for the US implementation of the FATF-derived cross-border 'travel rule' recordkeeping requirements for funds and value transfers.
formal_definition: 31 CFR 1010.100(e): the Currency and Foreign Transactions Reporting Act, its amendments, and other statutes relating to the subject matter of that Act 'have come to be referred to as the Bank Secrecy Act,' codified in relevant part at 12 U.S.C. 1829b, 1951-1959 and 31 U.S.C. 5311-5314 and 5316-5332.
